title: Race-lap set encoder over the whole field context
summary: Each row is not independent: pit windows open relative to the whole race state, traffic, and who else may box.
rationale: Each row is not independent: pit windows open relative to the whole race state, traffic, and who else may box. Set Transformer style models are built for permutation-invariant interactions among items in a set, which matches the per-lap grid snapshot better than flat row-wise tabular models.
implementation_hint: For each `Year+Race+LapNumber`, treat all cars on that lap as a set with per-car features; predict each car’s next-lap pit probability using a small set encoder plus a per-row head. Keep it lightweight and use it as a specialist whose OOF predictions are blended with GBDTs rather than replacing the main model.
expected_effect: May capture synchronized strategy states and relative pressure patterns that row models miss, especially around mass pit windows or weather-driven collective behavior.
risk: This is architecturally heavier and can underperform trees if the grouping logic or padding/masking is sloppy; strict race-group validation is essential.
